title: Race-Lap Pit Wave Prior
summary: F1 pit timing often clusters into strategy windows because teams react to the same tyre degradation, traffic, and undercut incentives.
rationale: F1 pit timing often clusters into strategy windows because teams react to the same tyre degradation, traffic, and undercut incentives. The best prior solutions use individual tyre/wear signals but do not appear to encode how common pitting is for the field at a given race lap or nearby laps.
implementation_hint: Create leakage-safe out-of-fold target-rate features by Year/Race/LapNumber, Race/LapNumber, RaceProgress bin, Compound/Stint/LapNumber bin, and +/-1 lap smoothed windows. For test, fit encoders on all train with smoothing toward global mean; for CV, compute within folds only. Add counts/frequencies and smoothed pit hazard, not raw target leakage.
expected_effect: May improve AUC by capturing collective strategy waves that individual car features miss, especially at canonical one-stop/two-stop windows.
risk: High leakage risk if target rates are computed before splitting; CV may inflate if same race-lap groups appear in both train and validation without proper OOF encoding.
